answer:
We place triangle \(ABC\) with \(B=(0,0),\;C=(17,0)\).  From \(AB=26,\;AC=25\) we obtain \(A=(10,24)\).  
The orthocenter is the intersection of the altitudes:  
\[
H=\left(10,\frac{35}{12}\right).
\]  
The feet of the altitudes are  
\[
D=(10,0),\quad 
E=\left(\frac{9792}{625},\frac{2856}{625}\right),\quad 
F=\left(\frac{425}{169},\frac{1020}{169}\right).
\]  
Line \(EF\) meets \(BC\) (the \(x\)-axis) at \(X\).  A direct computation gives  
\[
X=\left(\frac{170}{3},0\right).
\]  
Line \(AX\) has direction \((35,-18)\); a parametrisation is  
\[
(x,y)=(10,24)+t(35,-18).
\]  
The circumcircle \(\Omega\) has equation \(x^{2}+y^{2}-17x-\frac{253}{12}y=0\).  
Substituting the param gives \(1549t^{2}-\frac{759}{2}t=0\); the non‑zero root is  
\[
t=\frac{759}{3098}.
\]  
Hence the second intersection \(Q\) of \(AX\) with \(\Omega\) is  
\[
Q=\left(\frac{57545}{3098},\frac{30345}{1549}\right).
\]  
The nine‑point circle (circle \(DEF\)) is the image of \(\Omega\) under the homothety with centre \(H\) and factor \(\frac12\).  Therefore the midpoint of \(HQ\) lies on this circle.  Because \(H\) lies inside the nine‑point circle, the segment \(HQ\) meets the circle exactly at this midpoint.  Thus  
\[
P=\frac{H+Q}{2}=\left(\frac{88525}{6196},\frac{418355}{37176}\right).
\]  
Now compute the required ratio.  Let  
\[
\mathbf{u}=Q-H,\qquad \mathbf{v}=H-E.
\]  
Then \(P=\frac{H+Q}{2}=H+\frac12\mathbf{u}\), so  
\[
PE=\Bigl|\frac12\mathbf{u}-\mathbf{e}\Bigr|=\frac12\bigl|\mathbf{u}-2\mathbf{e}\bigr|,
\qquad PQ=\frac12|Q-H|=\frac12|\mathbf{u}|.
\]  
Hence  
\[
\frac{PQ}{PE}= \frac{|\mathbf{u}|}{|\mathbf{u}-2\mathbf{e}|}.
\]  
We have  
\[
\mathbf{u}=\left(\frac{26565}{3098},\frac{25\cdot12397}{18588}\right),\qquad 
\mathbf{e}=E-H=\left(-\frac{3542}{625},-\frac{12397}{7500}\right).
\]  
Write \(d=1549\) (so \(3098=2d,\;18588=12d\)).  Then  
\[
\mathbf{u}=\left(\frac{26565}{2d},\frac{25\cdot12397}{12d}\right),\qquad 
\mathbf{e}=\left(-\frac{3542}{625},-\frac{12397}{12\cdot625}\right).
\]  
Set \(\mathbf{w}=\mathbf{u}-2\mathbf{e}\).  After clearing denominators we obtain  
\[
\mathbf{w}=\left(-\frac{5343107}{1936250},\;\frac{931783314}{69705000}\right).
\]  
The squared lengths are  
\[
|\mathbf{u}|^{2}= \frac{121458677725}{345513744},\qquad 
|\mathbf{w}|^{2}= \frac{1005799310240725}{5398652250000}.
\]  
Now \(345513744 = 144\cdot1549^{2}\) and \(5398652250000 = 144\cdot1549^{2}\cdot15625\).  Hence  
\[
\frac{|\mathbf{u}|^{2}}{|\mathbf{w}|^{2}}
= \frac{121458677725\cdot15625}{1005799310240725}
= \frac{125^{2}}{91^{2}}.
\]  
Therefore  
\[
\frac{PQ}{PE}= \frac{125}{91}.
\]  
The numbers \(125\) and \(91\) are coprime, so \(a=125,\;b=91\) and  
\[
a+b = 216.
\]  

\[
\boxed{216}
\]
